claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to understand your situation. 

**First, let me ask about the child or children you're claiming for:**

1. **Do you have a child or children you're responsible for?** If yes, please tell me:
   - The name(s) of the child(ren)
   - Their age(s)

This will help me determine which eligibility path we need to follow, as the requirements differ based on whether the child is:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old (where education/training status matters)
- 20 or over

Please share these details so we can continue with your assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 13 years old (under 16), that's a good start. Now I need to check a few more things:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

This is just a standard check - for most 13-year-olds, they either don't work or might have very limited part-time work like a paper round or weekend job.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's what I expected for a 13-year-old in school.

**Next question: Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, I'm asking if Alex receives benefits like:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Or similar benefits claimed in his own name

For most 13-year-olds in school, the answer would be no, but I need to check this as part of the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now let me check about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

Again, for a 13-year-old in regular school, this would typically be "no," but I need to confirm. Some apprenticeship types can affect Child Benefit eligibility depending on where you live (England, Scotland, or Wales have different rules).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any apprenticeship. He's just doing regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now we need to check about your residency status. This is an important requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common situation)

2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme

3. **You have pre-settled status** and meet one of these conditions:
   - You're working and earning (or expect to earn) above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months
   - You're actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
   - You have sufficient resources to support yourself and your family (and NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
   - You're studying and have sufficient resources
   - You're a family member of an EEA or Swiss national with right to reside

4. **You're a Crown servant living abroad** or living in certain qualifying countries

**Which of these applies to you?** Most people qualify simply because they normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't live in the UK - I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me check if you fall under any of the special circumstances that would still make you eligible.

**Can you tell me more about your situation abroad?**

Specifically:

1. **Are you a Crown servant?** (This includes:
   - Working for the UK government overseas
   - Member of HM Forces serving abroad
   - Working for certain UK public bodies overseas)

2. **Or are you living in certain qualifying countries?** There are some specific circumstances where you can claim while abroad.

3. **Or have you recently moved abroad?** (There may be temporary provisions)

Please let me know which, if any, of these apply to your situation so I can determine if you meet the residency requirements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just moved abroad for work - it's a regular private sector job.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to have to tell you this, but based on your circumstances, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit**.

## Assessment Result: **INELIGIBLE**

### Why you're not eligible:

While Alex meets all the requirements as a qualifying child (he's 13 years old, in regular school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

Child Benefit requires that the claimant (the person claiming) must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Being a Crown servant working abroad
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying conditions)
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you've moved abroad for a regular private sector job and don't fall into any of the special categories, you don't meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

### What this means:

Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit while living and working abroad in your current circumstances, even though Alex would otherwise be an eligible child.

### Possible options to consider:

- If you move back to the UK and normally live here again, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex
- If someone else (such as Alex's other parent or guardian) normally lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, they may be able to claim instead

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask or reapply once you're back in the UK.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship, which would normally qualify him as an eligible child. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad for a regular private sector job and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in qualifying countries. Since the claimant fails to meet the residency requirements, they are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has a qualifying child (Alex, age 13) who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad for private sector work without any of the special qualifying circumstances (Crown servant status, EU Settlement Scheme status, etc.). Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exceptions, which this claimant does not.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
